assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9.
The recognised corresponding receivable is a financial asset or monetary item arising from an intragroup dividend.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions, including certain foreign currency risks.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 guidance on hedges of foreign currency risk arising from net investments in foreign operations in consolidated financial statements.
